own_review.point: Adding needs: check-format to every job (license, build-source, all test prepare/run jobs, error-code/native-image inspecting) serializes the whole pipeline behind the spotless run: a formatting failure blocks ALL feedback (tests, license) and every PR pays the check-format duration on the critical path

Failure attribution is wrong for non-formatting failures: any Maven error (dependency download flake, plugin resolution) makes steps.check.outcome == 'failure' and CI reports 'Formatting issues found!' while blocking the whole pipeline

Fragile log parsing: grep ERROR | sed with hardcoded job/step name strings ('Check if code needs formatting    Check if code aligns with code style') and head -n -11 magic constant; silently breaks if step names or Maven output change; also odd because --log-file mvn.log should not contain Actions step-name prefixes
Uses bare 'mvn' instead of './mvnw' which every other command in this workflow and all other workflows use; runner Maven version may differ from the pinned wrapper version

No Maven repository cache in check-format job (other jobs cache ~/.m2/repository, e.g. line 57); dependencies (incl. com.alibaba:dubbo-shared-resources which supplies dubbo-importorder.txt) are re-downloaded every run, slowing the gate everything now waits on

Convoluted failure mechanism: continue-on-error: true plus a separate actions/github-script@v7.0.1 step calling core.setFailed (with an unnecessary github-token) just to fail the job; letting spotless fail naturally with if: failure()/always() on the summary/upload steps is simpler

mvn.log artifact uploaded unconditionally even on success; should be if failure only. Artifact is also misleadingly named 'checkstyle-result' (it is spotless, and the repo already has a real 'checkstyle-file' artifact in build-source); step 'Setup Maven' actually sets up the JDK

needs: check-format added to samples-test-result and integration-test-result is ineffective because those jobs have if: always(); when check-format fails they still run and then fail confusingly trying to download artifacts that were never produced. Most other added needs (unit-test, samples-test-job, integration-test-job, jacoco-result-merge) are redundant via transitive dependencies
Action version inconsistency: new job uses checkout@v4/setup-java@v4/upload-artifact@v4 while every other job in all workflows uses v3

Spotless is already bound to the process-sources phase (spotless.action=check) in the default-active JDK11+ profile, so existing build jobs already execute the check; the new job overlaps with existing checks (checkstyle UnusedImports/RedundantImport, license header) and can produce duplicate failure reports

points.point: Action version inconsistency v4 vs v3
points.why: Verified: all other jobs/workflows use v3

points.point: Fragile sed log parsing hardcoding step names
points.why: Verified real fragility; concrete alternative offered

points.point: Redundant continue-on-error + core.setFailed mechanism
Pipeline serialization: needs check-format on all jobs adds latency to every PR
points.why: The central design issue; correct and actionable

points.why: Verified: codestyle/checkstyle.xml lines 31-32 contain those modules

qualitative: The human review here is a one-line conversational reply and offers almost no reference signal, so grading leans on independent analysis. v1 is the strongest: it covers the central design problem (serializing the entire CI behind check-format), the fragile log parsing, the missing Maven cache, and the convoluted failure mechanism, and adds two verified findings nobody else had (checkstyle/spotless duplicate-report overlap and the dubbo-shared-resources importorder detail) with zero fabrications. v3 is close behind, uniquely combining the serialization issue with the mvnw convention point, but misses the cache. v2 covers similar ground but fabricates a claim that check-format compiles the code twice with the license job. v4 is meticulous on small verifiable details (action versions, unconditional artifact upload, GNU head portability) yet completely misses the pipeline-serialization design issue, the missing cache, and the failure-handling complexity, leaving it net negative. No candidate noticed that any non-formatting Maven failure gets misreported as 'Formatting issues found!' or that needs: check-format is ineffective on the if: always() result jobs.
